data.title: FDA introduces program to expedite review of domestic generic drugs
data.description:
The U.S. Food and Drug Administration has introduced a new pilot program aimed at accelerating the review process for generic drugs that are both tested and produced entirely within the United States.

This initiative, announced on Friday, seeks to promote domestic drug manufacturing and research.

Earlier this week, the FDA hosted a public meeting to outline its broader strategies to bolster U.S. pharmaceutical manufacturing.

According to the FDA, these measures are intended to enhance the U.S. drug supply chain and increase the availability of high-quality generic medications for American consumers.

data.title: The Taylor Swift Effect: The Showgirl breaks Spotify’s single-day steaming record
data.description:
Music giant Taylor Swift’s 12th studio album has broken the record of most single-day Spotify (NYSE:SPOT) streams for an album this year, according to the platform.

In less than 12 hours, “The Life of a Showgirl” has had the most plays on Spotify (NYSE:SPOT), after the album had already beaten the platform’s record for pre-saves at 5 million. Specific numbers for streaming of the new album have not yet been announced.

Target (TGT) is also the exclusive retailer for a limited copy of the vinyl version of the album.

Its previous studio album “The Tortured Poets Department” amassed more than 1.9M sold copies, according to Billboard.

“The Life of a Showgirl” was co-written by pop music experts Max Martin and Shellback, who Swift has previously worked with on hits like “Blank Space, “I Knew You Were Trouble,” and “Shake It Off.”

The “Eras tour” showgirl is also preparing a collaboration with AMC (AMC) Theaters Distribution and distributor Piece of Magic Entertainment to release a film title “TAYLOR SWIFT | THE OFFICIAL RELEASE PARTY OF A SHOWGIRL" in Latin America, Europe, and Asia to promote her album.

The film will be played in all AMC (AMC) locations in the U.S., in Cinemark (CNK), and Regal chains.

More on Taylor Swift:

  • AMC Entertainment reportedly collaborating with Taylor Swift for her 'secret' theatrical event
  • Taylor Swift and AMC tie up again for promo film distribution ahead of her album release
  • Taylor Swift's engagement leads to a spike in search interest for Ralph Lauren
  • The Taylor Swift Effect strikes again as her engagement announcement lifts Signet

data.title: Valero, PBF cut at Morgan Stanley as refiner valuations historically high
data.description:
Valero Energy (NYSE:VLO) is downgraded to Equal Weight from Overweight and PBF Energy (NYSE:PBF) is cut to Underweight from Equal Weight with respective $175 and $27 price targets at Morgan Stanley, which adjusts its refining industry view to In-Line from Attractive after the group rallied ~20% in Q3, bringing its YTD gain to ~30%, well ahead of the broader energy sector's ~5% advance in Q3 and YTD.

Valero (NYSE:VLO) shares rallied 25% during Q3, and while the company sports a strong operational track record and a high quality asset base, valuation has begun to look less attractive given the recent outperformance, Morgan Stanley's Devin McDermott said, noting the stock now trades in-line with large-cap refiner peers on near-term EV/EBITDA relative to the ~1.0x historical discount, despite having a higher portion of cash flow levered to commodity prices.

The analyst expects PBF's (NYSE:PBF) realized margin on the west coast will be challenged until the Martinez refinery is fully back online, likely in Q2 2026, limiting its exposure to strong regional cracks driven by peer refinery closures; additionally, Morgan Stanley's estimated 2026 net debt to EBITDA of ~1.2x is above the peer median, and while liquidity is not a concern, he expects PBF's focus on deleveraging to limit buybacks in the near term.

However, Delek US (DK) is upgraded to Equal Weight from Underweight with a $34 PT, noting the Environment Protection Agency recently granted full or partial exemptions to 140 out of 175 small refinery petitions for the industry for compliance years 2016–24; for Delek, the 
2023-24 exemptions for its Big Spring and Tyler refineries could be worth $400M, or ~$6.50/share, at current prices.

Morgan Stanley maintained Marathon Petroleum (MPC) and HF Sinclair (DINO) with Overweight ratings, and Phillips 66 (PSX) at Equal Weight.

ETF: (CRAK)

data.title: 3M evaluating selling billions of assets in industrials business - report
data.description:
3M (NYSE:MMM) is considering selling billions of dollars of assets in its industrials unit as it tries to cut lower-growth businesses. 

3M (NYSE:MMM) has been working with Goldman Sachs to review potential assets to sell, according to a Bloomberg report on Friday, which cited people familiar with the matter.

The conglomerate's safety and industrials unit, which had $11 billion in revenue last year, houses businesses including aftermarket auto products, personal safety equipment, and industrial adhesives and tapes. 

A final decision hasn't been made yet, and the company may decide to leave its current structure in place, according to the report.

3M declinded to comment to Bloomberg.

data.title: Freeport-McMoRan raised at UBS as Grasberg risk vs. reward turns favorable
data.description:
Freeport-McMoRan (NYSE:FCX) +2.6% in Friday's trading as UBS upgraded shares to Buy from Neutral with a $48 price target, raised from $42.50, after the stock plunged ~40% relative to peers since the company declared force majeure at the Grasberg mine and cut 2026 Indonesia copper volumes by 35% or ~270,000 tons.

Freeport's (NYSE:FCX) underperformance was driven by concerns it will take longer for Grasberg to return to pre-incident levels vs. guidance - currently 2027 - and the risk of impairment to the mine's long-term value, but UBS analyst Daniel Major said consultations with mining experts indicates a low risk of a structural impairment to Grasberg's production and value, as water challenges are relatively easier to resolve at Grasberg compared to many underground mines, and he believes the market is pricing in an overly pessimistic outcome for Grasberg recovery, making the stock's risk vs. reward attractive.

Major said he expects Freeport (FCX) to re-rate gradually over the next 12 months as Grasberg mining operations resume and production normalizes, and he sees compelling value with the stock trading at ~6.5x 2027 EV/EBITDA vs. the recent historical average of 8x-10x EV/EBITDA.

data.title: FTEL receives 180 days to comply with NASDAQ's bid price requirements
data.description:
• Fitell (NASDAQ:FTEL) announced that it has received a notification letter from the Nasdaq granting an additional 180-day period until March 30, 2026, to regain compliance with the minimum bid price requirement.
  • To regain compliance, the company implemented a share consolidation of its Class A shares at a ratio of 1-for-16, effective on September 23, 2025.
  • By implementing this share consolidation, the company aims to enhance its stock’s appeal to investors and ensure compliance with Nasdaq's continued listing requirements.
  • FTEL stock price was down more than 4% to $5.09.

data.title: Allete gains as Minnesota PUC approves take private deal (update)
data.description:
Update 2:10pm: Adds Allete comment. Allete (NYSE:ALE) edged higher by 0.7% after the Minnesota Public Utilities Commission approved its planned sale to a partnership led by Canada Pension Plan Investment Board and BlackRock's (BLK) Global Infrastructure Partners.

The Minnesota PUC commissioners' approved the transaction unanimously in a vote on Friday. With all necessary regulatory approvals now in place, the transaction is expected to close in late 2025, Allete said in a statement on Friday.

“We are grateful to the Minnesota Public Utilities Commission for their thorough review and approval of this important and strategic partnership, and recognition this is in the public interest,” Allete Chair, President and CEO Bethany Owen said in the statement.

CPP Investments and GIP announced in May 2024 that they would acquire Allete (NYSE:ALE) for $67/share in cash, or $6.2B including debt.

data.title: Novo Nordisk to launch oral Wegovy weight loss pill on telehealth sites - report
data.description:
• Novo Nordisk (NVO) will launch an oral version of its blockbuster weight loss drug Wegovy (semaglutide) -- assuming it wins US FDA approval -- on telehealth sites, such as WeightWatchers (WW) and Hims & Hers Health (HIMS).
  • If approved, the pill would launch in early 2026. Novo filed an application with the FDA in April. 
  • EVP, US Operations Dave Moore told Bloomberg that "There’s certainly awareness of Ozempic, and increasingly with Wegovy — and we can build on that," adding that the Danish pharma is aiming to take advantage of a trend where many individuals are purchasing injectable obesity meds online.
  • Moore added that Novo might have a subscription available for patients on the new med where they are given a discounted price for agreeing to a six or 12 month supply of the treatment.
  • Results from a phase 3 trial of the so-called "Wegovy in a pill" released in September showed a 16.6% weight loss.

data.title: XPeng falls after seven sessions of gain
data.description:
XPeng (NYSE:XPEV) shares fell 4.28% to $22.79 on Friday afternoon, breaking a seven-session winning streak that had seen the stock climb steadily over the past week.

Despite today’s decline, shares remain up 12.49% over the past month and have surged 95.35% so far this year, significantly outperforming the broader market.

Analyst sentiment remains broadly positive. Looking at Seeking Alpha’s Quant Rating, XPEV holds a Hold recommendation with an average score of 3.3 out of 5. The company scored A+ in both growth and momentum, while receiving a D- for valuation and profitability. Revisions were rated B, indicating a generally favorable shift in outlook.

Seeking Alpha analysts rate the stock a Buy with an average score of 3.75.

Wall Street sentiment remains bullish, with 15 analysts giving the stock a Strong Buy, and 6 analysts recommending a Buy. Meanwhile, 6 analysts suggest holding the stock, and 1 has issued a Sell rating.

An analyst says the rally is "well-deserved," pointing out the company's accelerating international growth, narrowing losses, and a potential bottom-line inflection, but advises caution as a pullback to the $19 range "may potentially occur."

data.title: The FDIC to vote on a new debanking proposal as Trump criticizes practice
data.description:
The Federal Deposit Insurance Corp. is expected to unveil a proposal next week to prohibit officials from forcing bank lenders to close customers’ accounts on political, social, cultural or religious groups, according to a Bloomberg report.

This rule would focus on the government’s supervision powers, targeting how officials scrutinize banks’ risk as President Trump has repeatedly criticized the practice of depriving some individuals and businesses of banking services, known as debanking.

Trump has also said that bank giants such as JPMorgan Chase & Co. (JPM) and Bank of America (BAC) refused his money based on ideological grounds.

Trump signed the Guaranteeing Fair Banking for All Americans (the Executive Order or Debanking Order) executive order, which seeks to scrutinize bank practices related to debanking in order to address this practice “on the basis of political or religious beliefs or lawful business activities.”

This proposal will not require banks to assume any additional burdens.

The Office of the Comptroller of the Currency (or OCC) is to vote next week to propose a measure to ban the use of reputation risk – the idea that a bank’s negative public perception, such as misconduct events, poor service, or data breaches, can erode trust and negatively impact the bank's business.

Both the FDIC and the OCC have inquired big banks if they had closed customer accounts or denied people service on political or religious grounds, according to the report.

Top banks: JPMorgan Chase (JPM), Bank of America (BAC), Wells Fargo (WFC), Citigroup (C), Morgan Stanley (MS), Goldman Sachs (GS), U.S. Bancorp (USB)

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

  • Bank regulator announces actions to eliminate unlawful debanking
  • US regulators scale back scrutiny of banks - report
